Title: Pengerang Energy Complex appoints China's CNCEC as EPCC contractor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pengerang Energy Complex Sdn Bhd (PEC) has awarded the engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning (EPCC) contract for its downstream petrochemical facility in Johor to China National Chemical Engineering Co Ltd (CNCEC).
PEC said CNCEC was selected following a comprehensive evaluation process that assessed technical expertise, financial strength and project execution capabilities.
The company cited CNCEC's experience in delivering large-scale energy and petrochemical projects, as well as its familiarity with Honeywell UOP technologies that form part of the project's process design.
The award comes as PEC progresses through the final approval stages with global export credit agencies and project finance lenders.
In a statement, PEC said the appointment reflects the project's continued progress towards construction and underscores its commitment to delivering a world-scale facility.
The contract award also strengthens the strategic partnership between PEC and CNCEC, with both parties expected to explore future collaboration opportunities in planned energy and petrochemical developments.
The development follows reports that CNCEC was set to replace Italy-based Maire SpA as the project's main contractor after the project experienced several timeline revisions amid financing challenges and geopolitical uncertainties.
The US$5.32 billion Pengerang Energy Complex, developed by Singapore-based ChemOne Group, is one of the major investments within the Pengerang Integrated Petroleum Complex, Malaysia's largest petrochemical hub.
Once completed, the integrated condensate splitter and aromatics complex is expected to produce 2.7 million tonnes of aromatics, 3.9 million tonnes of petroleum products and 50,000 tonnes of hydrogen annually for regional and global markets.
Industry observers remain positive on the long-term prospects of the project and the wider PIPC ecosystem, supported by growing petrochemical demand in Asia and Malaysia's strategic location along major shipping routes, although financing conditions, cost inflation and energy market volatility continue to present execution risks.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man found dead outside HTAA believed to have fallen from hospital building
Article:
KUANTAN: A man found unconscious in front of the main lobby of Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA) on June 6 is believed to have fallen from the hospital building.
Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the case had been classified as sudden death.
"Investigations found that the victim had previously received treatment for mental health issues at the hospital.
"No criminal elements have been identified," he said when contacted.
On June 6, the Pahang Health Department said the
man was found lying unconscious
outside the hospital building about 10am before medical personnel confirmed his death.
The department also clarified that the deceased was neither a patient nor a staff member of the hospital.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chow non-committal on fresh extension for Jelutong reclamation project
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow remains non-committal as to whether Penang will grant another extension for the controversial Jelutong reclamation project, citing an ongoing review.
His response comes amid mounting pressure from Protect Karpal Singh Drive Action Committee (ProtectKarpal), which challenged him to honour an earlier pledge and clarify whether any further extension will be granted to PLB Engineering Bhd for the RM1 billion Jelutong landfill rehabilitation and coastal reclamation project off Karpal Singh Drive.
ProtectKarpal has been
actively campaigning
against the project.
Chow said the state's consideration of the environmental impact assessment (EIA) application remains ongoing and that no decision has been reached.
"The process is still ongoing. We are aware of the various allegations and concerns raised by non-governmental organisations, but any decision must be based on a proper evaluation of all available information.
"We are undertaking that assessment now because any decision carries implications, particularly from a financial perspective," he told newsmen here today.
Repeatedly pressed on whether the state would approve a fresh extension, Chow declined to give a direct answer, saying only that an announcement would be made once a decision has been finalised.
"Once a decision is made, we will announce it," he added.
Asked when the matter could be resolved, Chow said discussions involving the relevant agencies had only taken place yesterday, with their findings set to form part of the state's deliberations.
He added that the state Environment Committee had met with the Department of Environment (DoE), and the outcome of those discussions would be tabled to the state executive council tomorrow.
Chow also confirmed that the DoE is still processing submissions from the company involved.
The issue has resurfaced following renewed calls from ProtectKarpal, which argued that the project could have significant environmental and community impacts.
It urged the state government to stand by previous assurances that no further extensions would be entertained.
The DoE had recently rejected the project's Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) report, halting the project.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PN may be 'put to sleep' if divisions persist, say analysts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The breakdown of cooperation between Pas and Bersatu has triggered a wider power struggle over the future of Perikatan Nasional (PN), with analysts warning that the opposition coalition could be "put to sleep" if internal divisions deepen.
Universiti Malaya political analyst Dr Mohammad Tawfik Yaakub said PN is no longer sustainable without both Pas and Bersatu, but the latest split is likely to spark a contest between the two parties to determine who ultimately controls the PN brand.
He said it is unlikely that both parties will continue under the same coalition umbrella following the termination of ties, adding that PN has already become a recognised political "brand" among voters.
"I believe there will be a struggle over PN between Pas and Bersatu to ensure only one party effectively dominates the coalition moving forward.
"It is impossible for both to remain under the same coalition after ties have been officially ended," he told the New Straits Times.
He said Bersatu would be at greater risk if it failed to remain within PN, as the coalition has largely depended on Pas' grassroots machinery and electoral strength.
"If both parties end up contesting against each other in elections, it will open the door for their opponents to benefit."
Tawfik said PN could eventually be "put to sleep" if neither Pas nor Bersatu is willing to step aside or restructure the coalition, forcing both parties to pursue alternative political alignments.
He added that the fragmentation of the opposition would ultimately weaken its ability to form a unified front against the ruling coalition.
He said a divided opposition would struggle to present itself as a credible "government-in-waiting", making it easier for the ruling parties to maintain an electoral advantage in the next general election.
Universiti Teknologi Malaysia Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities lecturer Associate Professor Dr Mazlan Ali said if Pas leaves the coalition, it could lead to its restructuring, with Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin potentially continuing to lead the coalition alongside remaining component parties.
"This means Pas may leave PN, while Muhyiddin could continue to lead PN with Bersatu, Gerakan and other component parties."
However, he said PN would be significantly weakened without Pas, which he described as a heavyweight component with strong grassroots machinery.
"If Pas leaves, PN will lose a major partner. Pas is a strong party with a large membership and strong grassroots machinery," he said.
He said Pas' strongholds in the east coast and northern states had been complemented by Bersatu's leadership, allowing PN to expand into states such as Kedah, Perlis, Selangor, Melaka, Johor and Negri Sembilan.
"Without this partnership, Pas may revert to its core base, while PN will no longer enjoy the same national reach."
Mazlan said parties such as MIPP and Gerakan were likely to remain aligned with Bersatu under PN, though their influence remained limited.
Meanwhile, Universiti Sains Malaysia political analyst Professor Datuk Dr Sivamurugan Pandian said Pas' decision to end cooperation with Bersatu could mark the beginning of a broader realignment within the opposition bloc.
He said the key question is not merely about the breakup itself, but which party is able to quickly construct a new narrative and coalition ahead of the next general election.
"Pas' decision to end cooperation with Bersatu is not just a story of separation between two parties, but potentially the beginning of a political realignment within the opposition. The question is not who has split, but who can quickly build a new narrative and coalition to face the upcoming election."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Good drivers can get 10pct insurance premium discount - Loke
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Motorists with good driving records can now enjoy insurance premium discounts of up to 10 per cent under the newly launched Cermat Madani programme.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the initiative, introduced by the Transport Ministry in collaboration with 10 insurance and takaful providers, rewards drivers with low-risk profiles based on their driving records and traffic summonses.
Motorists renewing their road tax through the MyJPJ application would undergo a risk assessment by participating insurers and takaful operators.
"Vehicle owners who meet the criteria will be given an additional discount or rebate on their insurance premium," he told a press conference here today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Saifuddin: Any alliance with Pas ultimately ends in split
Article:
BATU PAHAT: Pas' decision to cut ties with Bersatu is nothing new as the party had previously severed relationships with other parties and coalitions, says Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail.
The Pakatan Harapan secretary-general said the decision made by the Pas central working committee last night was a mere repeat of a long-standing pattern in national politics.
He said every political cooperation entered into by Pas would inevitably end in a split.
He said, looking at Pas' cooperation with Semangat 46, PH itself, then with Umno through Muafakat Nasional 1.0, and later Bersatu, all suffered the same fate.
"Although the reason given is always the same, which is the unification of the ummah, all of that is based on Pas' own interpretation and viewpoint. That is why I say politics is dynamic.
"The fundamental principles of politics in Islam are to uphold justice, commend good and forbid evil, call for goodness, and sow love and unity. Those are the principles of Islamic politics.
"Who is next? Anyone who aligns with Pas will end up in a divorce. The only question is whether it is a first, second, or third
talak
(repudiation)," he said.
Saifuddin, who is home minister, told this to reporters after attending the "Citra Negara Programme: Safe School" function at the Advanced Technology Training Centre (Adtec), here.
Also present were Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad, Batu Pahat Adtec director Dr Muhammad Yusof, and Batu Pahat police chief Assistant Commissioner Shahrulanuar Mushaddat Abdullah Sani.
Yesterday, Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang had said the party's central working committee had decided to
sever ties
with Bersatu.
He had said the meeting endorsed Pas' move to explore a new form of political understanding and electoral cooperation for the unification of the ummah ahead of the upcoming state elections and the 16th General Election.
Pas information chief Ahmad Fadhli Shaari later said the party would
remain in Perikatan Nasional
.
In a response today, Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin said
Bersatu was disappointed
in the decision but would also remain in PN.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 18 reports lodged over reckless Triton driver who damaged vehicles on NSE
Article:
BUKIT MERTAJAM: Police have received 18 reports from motorists whose vehicles were damaged after being struck by a Mitsubishi Triton driven recklessly along the North-South Expressway (NSE) near here yesterday.
The incident has gone viral on social media.
Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said preliminary investigations showed that 18 vehicles were damaged in a series of collisions involving the 29-year-old driver.
He said the suspect had been remanded for four days from today to assist investigations. https://www.nst.com.my/news/regional/2026/06/1458738/triton-driver-viral-reckless-driving-clip-remanded-four-days-watch
"Further investigations are ongoing. The suspect, who was arrested shortly after the incident yesterday, has been remanded for four days," he said when contacted.
The case is being investigated under Section 12(3) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 and Section 44(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for driving under the influence of drugs causing an accident.
Police received a report at 10.05am yesterday on a crash involving a Mitsubishi Triton on the NSE.
Initial investigations found the suspect allegedly drove recklessly from Sungai Dua towards Penang Island, colliding with multiple vehicles along the route.
He was eventually detained by members of the public on the Butterworth–Kulim Expressway (BKE) near a Caltex station in Seberang Jaya before being handed over to police.
A search uncovered a sling bag containing a transparent packet with crystal-like substances believed to be methamphetamine, as well as another packet suspected to contain ketamine.
Urine tests showed the suspect was positive for methamphetamine and morphine.
Police records also revealed that he has 15 prior drug-related offences and two criminal records.
The case has sparked strong reactions online, with many questioning how the suspect, given his extensive record, was still on the road.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Automatic citizenship policy for children born overseas to Malaysian mothers 'in final stages'
Article:
Pix/ Video Alias Abd Rani
BATU PAHAT: The Home Ministry is in the final stages of implementing a policy to grant automatic citizenship to children born overseas to Malaysian mothers married to foreign spouses.
Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said he was confident the matter would be resolved soon following the recent passage of the citizenship amendment bill in Parliament.
Under the new policy, the ministry will grant citizenship by operation of law, meaning Malaysian mothers will no longer have to go through lengthy processes to secure legal status for their children.
"At the ministry level, we have made all the necessary preparations, including equipping our overseas missions and preparing the forms, as applications or notifications can be made online.
"I am confident we can resolve this in the near future as we are at the very end of executing the citizenship amendments passed by Parliament recently," he said.
Previously, children born overseas to Malaysian mothers married to foreigners were required to apply for citizenship.
"We have amended this so that these children receive citizenship automatically by operation of law. This will have a major impact, especially on Malaysian mothers.
"They no longer need to apply to secure citizenship for their children. We are now in the final phase before finalising the implementation date of this new policy," he told a press conference after attending the Citra Negara programme here today.
Saifuddin also said that when he first took office, nearly 50,000 citizenship applications had been pending for more than a decade without any decision being communicated to applicants.
He said that during his three years at the ministry, he established a clear direction and shared it with ministry officials.
"If it required amendments in Parliament, we amended it. If it required improving citizenship procedures, we did it. Ultimately, of the 50,000
cases
, only slightly more than 2,000 remain," he said.
Previously, Saifuddin
announced
that the government had agreed to grant automatic citizenship by operation of law to children born overseas to Malaysian mothers married to non-citizen spouses.
The decision was made during a cabinet meeting after deliberating on and approving proposed amendments to the Federal Constitution under Part I Section 1(1)(d) and Section 1(1)(e), and Part II Section 1(b) and Section 1(c) of the Second Schedule.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang revenue surge driven by surplus, not tax hikes, says Chow
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang's rise in state revenue to more than RM600 million was driven by a large budget surplus in 2025 and not by increases in taxes or charges imposed on the people, says Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow.
Responding to criticism that the stronger revenue position was the result of higher taxes and fees burdening the rakyat, he said the latest figures reflected fiscal gains recorded last year.
"As at Dec 31, 2025, the figure was slightly above RM500 million. The increase was due to the large surplus recorded in 2025.
"This is not related to any tax increase. When did the quit rent rates go up? It only takes effect on Jan 1, 2026. The surplus has nothing to do with the increase in land tax," he told reporters after launching the Penang Career Discovery Day (PCDD) X Penang HR Forward Forum 2026.
Chow was responding to critics who questioned the state's financial position after he said Penang's consolidated revenue account had surpassed RM600 million and was on track towards the RM1 billion mark.
Critics had alleged that Penang residents had been hit by a series of increases since 2019, including higher assessment rates, water tariffs, parking charges and land tax hikes ranging from 29 per cent to 200 per cent.
They also compared Penang's financial performance with Johor, arguing that the southern state had strengthened its revenue through investment-driven growth rather than higher charges.
Yesterday, Chow said Penang's consolidated revenue reserves had rebounded to RM616 million,
dismissing claims
that the state was heading towards bankruptcy due to dwindling funds.
He also rejected allegations that Penang's financial position had deteriorated to the point where it could not meet its obligations, describing such claims as inaccurate and politically motivated.
Chow maintained that the state's revenue growth was driven by its budget surplus rather than tax increases.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Temporary relief centre opened for 110 Kampung Gok Kapur fire victims
Article:
KOTA BARU: The state Welfare Department has opened a temporary relief centre to accommodate 110 people  from 29 families whose homes were destroyed in a fire at Kampung Cina, here yesterday.
Its director, Che Samsuzuki Che Noh, said the centre was opened at Mukim Banggol mosque about 6.30pm yesterday.
So far, he said, the majority of the victims were relocated at the centre while the rest were taking shelters at their relative's homes.
"The registration process for all the affected families completed by 11pm last night.
"The incident was among the largest fires we have handled, involving 29 families whose houses were completely destroyed in the blaze.
"A total of 110 victims from various age groups have been registered at the centre including three persons with disabilities," he said when visiting the site today.
Che Samsuzuki said the department had also distributed emergency assistance kits to the affected victims.
"The financial aid from the department's welfare fund will be channelled to all affected families soon," he added.
He said the department would continue monitoring the welfare and immediate needs of the victims throughout their stay at the centre.
It was reported yesterday that 29 houses were
razed in the fire
.
However, no fatalities were reported in the incident.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PN turmoil exposes Samsuri's leadership failure, says Bersatu leader
Article:
SEREMBAN: The ongoing conflict within Perikatan Nasional (PN) is seen as a failure of Pas vice-president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar in his role as coalition chairman.
Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz claimed Samsuri has failed to effectively address a series of disputes, with several issues remaining unresolved since taking up the top PN post more than 100 days ago.
"Samsuri has also been relatively quiet in recent weeks on important matters, including political developments in Negri Sembilan, rakyat issues and internal tensions within PN.
"At the same time, this crisis has further exposed weaknesses in the coalition's leadership structure.
"Samsuri's position as Pas vice-president has limited his ability to act independently as PN chairman, contributing to Pas' decision to end its political cooperation with Bersatu, as announced by the party's central leadership last night," he said in a statement today.
He said the decision was expected, as Samsuri was seen as having failed to effectively manage and resolve internal conflicts, particularly those involving his own party.
"As Pas vice-president, he is constrained in making final decisions, as he must refer to the party president and the Syura council.
"This has resulted in many key PN issues not being resolved effectively through coalition channels," he said.
Tun Faisal said he understood that leaders from Bersatu, Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People's Party had sought a meeting with Samsuri to seek clarification over recent remarks made by Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang.
However, he said they have yet to receive a response.
"This raises questions about his effectiveness as PN chairman in ensuring the coalition functions properly, makes effective decisions, and manages disputes among its component parties," he added.
Pas has announced the
termination of its political cooperation with Bersatu
following a special meeting of the party's Central Working Committee yesterday.
Hadi said the committee endorsed a decision made by the Syura council on June 2 concerning policy, religious considerations and public interest aspects of Pas' political cooperation, based on the principle of Muslim unity.
He said the meeting also reviewed the current position and direction of Pas–Bersatu ties through reports, studies and assessments of the current political landscape.
Following the severed ties, Hadi said Pas would explore new forms of political understanding and electoral cooperation aimed at strengthening Muslim unity ahead of the upcoming state elections and the 16th general election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fitch cuts 2026 global growth forecast to 2.4pct on oil shock, raises China outlook
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Fitch Ratings has lowered its 2026 global growth forecast to 2.4 per cent from 2.6 per cent previously, citing the economic impact of the oil crisis triggered by the US-Iran war and the prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
According to Fitch, higher oil prices are fuelling inflation, squeezing household purchasing power and increasing costs for businesses, prompting widespread downgrades to growth forecasts across major economies.
The ratings agency cut its 2026 growth projections for the United States and eurozone by 0.3 and 0.4 percentage points respectively, to 1.9 per cent and 0.9 per cent.
Growth for emerging markets excluding China was also revised lower to 3.2 per cent. However, China bucked the trend, with Fitch raising its growth forecast by 0.3 percentage points to 4.6 per cent following stronger-than-expected first-quarter economic data and resilient exports.
South Korea's outlook was also upgraded, supported by robust global demand for technology products.
Fitch chief economist Brian Coulton said the oil price shock is weighing on global growth prospects and increasing downside risks, but a strong surge in global information technology investment is helping to offset some of the impact, particularly across Asia.
The agency has raised its average Brent crude oil price assumption for 2026 to US$87 per barrel from US$70 previously, reflecting the continued disruption caused by the Strait of Hormuz closure, which has now lasted 14 weeks.
Nevertheless, Fitch noted that the current shock is expected to be less severe than the oil crises of the 1970s, given the reduced importance of oil in the global economy.
The company additionally modelled a more adverse scenario in which oil prices average US$100 per barrel next year, accompanied by weaker financial markets and tighter credit conditions.
Under that scenario, US growth could slow to 0.8 per cent while growth in the eurozone and China could fall to 0.3 per cent and 3.4 per cent respectively.
Despite the energy shock, booming investment in artificial intelligence and technology continues to provide support for global trade and manufacturing activity.
According to Fitch, US information technology investment rose 18 per cent year-on-year in the first quarter, while global semiconductor sales surged 80 per cent in March.
Strong demand for technology products also boosted exports and economic growth in South Korea, Taiwan and China.
On monetary policy, Fitch expects central banks to remain cautious as higher oil prices add to inflation pressures.
The agency now forecasts the US Federal Reserve and the Bank of England to keep interest rates unchanged this year before resuming rate cuts in 2027.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police: 'Foreigners' flat' in Hulu Langat raided last year, 42 illegals detained
Article:
https://www.facebook.com/share/r/18YQWPkDKY/
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have clarified that a structure allegedly built illegally by foreigners in Hulu Langat, which recently went viral on social media, was raided last year, with 42 undocumented migrants detained.
Kajang police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said the clarification followed the circulation of a 47-second Facebook video on Tuesday showing a structure in Kampung Kenangan, Sungai Makau, allegedly erected illegally by foreigners.
He said police and the Selangor Immigration Department conducted an integrated enforcement operation, known as Ops Kutip, at the location on Feb 28, 2025.
"The operation resulted in the detention of 42 foreigners who failed to produce valid travel documents.
"They comprised 35 men, three women, two boys and two girls," he said in a statement today.
Naazron said the detainees were investigated by the Immigration Department under Section 51(3)(a) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 before being detained under Section 51(5)(b) of the same Act at an Immigration depot.
He added that investigators also issued a witness summons and a search form under Section 51(1) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 to a Malaysian who identified himself as the property owner's son to assist in investigations.
Naazron said enforcement action in the area would continue and urged those with information to contact 999 or the Kajang district police at 03-8911 4243.
The issue resurfaced after a 47-second Facebook video went viral, showing a man claiming that a flat-like structure in Kampung Kenangan, Sungai Makau, had been built and occupied by Rohingya migrants.
The video, which garnered thousands of views, sparked concern among social media users over the presence of undocumented foreigners in the area.
In the footage, the man questioned how the settlement was established and suggested it had grown into a sizeable community.
The video prompted numerous comments online, with users calling on the authorities to investigate the matter and verify the occupants' legal status.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Khazanah CIO: Malaysia must move beyond manufacturing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia needs to develop its economy from a manufacturing and industrial production position to capturing the wealth of industry.
Khazanah Nasional Bhd chief investment officer Datuk Hisham Hamdan said Malaysia was among the countries that benefited when markets sought for competitive destinations for manufacturing capital and production across Asia when the Japanese yen appreciated in 1985.
However, he said the global landscape changed significantly after China's entry into the World Trade Organisation (WTO) in 2001.
As manufacturing activities increasingly concentrated in China, Malaysia's competitive advantage based on cost alone became harder to sustain.
This pushed Malaysia to the middle-income trap when it absorbed "foreign assembly functions more successfully" than scaling homegrown intellectual property.
"While Malaysia's gross national income (GNI) per capita of US$11,650 as of 2024 remains below the World Bank's high-income status threshold of US%13,935, South Korea has climbed to roughly three times Malaysia's level at a GNI per capita of US$36,750.
"International benchmarks tell a similar story where South Korea represents about 26 per cent of MSCI Asia ex Japan index weight, relative to about one per cent for Malaysia.
"The divergence reflects a deeper challenge in Malaysia's productive capabilities," he said in the Khazanah Report 2025.
Hisham also pointed to Malaysia's ranking in the Harvard Growth Lab's Economic Complexity Index (ECI), which ranked 32nd globally, two places lower than a decade ago.
Countries such as Romania, Lithuania and Croatia overtook Malaysia, while Vietnam has made notable gains in expanding its productive capabilities.
Hisham noted that Malaysia's biggest challenge is that much of the value generated locally ultimately accrues elsewhere.
While multinational companies manufacture products in Malaysia and employ local workers, intellectual property, strategic decisions and capital returns often remain concentrated at overseas headquarters.
He described this as a "hotel economy", where significant activity takes place within a country but the highest-value transactions and decisions belong to others.
"The solution is not to close the hotel. Foreign investment and global supply chains remain essential.
"But Malaysia must participate more meaningfully in the value being created, not merely provide the location where it happens," he said.
To support Malaysia's next stage of development, Khazanah launched Dana Impak, a RM6 billion catalytic capital platform under the Finance Ministry's GEAR-uP initiative.
"This is not capital deployed for financial returns alone. It is catalytic capital deployed with strategic intent," Hisham said.
Among the key initiatives under Dana Impak is Jelawang Capital, which supports Malaysia's venture capital ecosystem through programmes such as the Emerging Fund Managers Programme and the Regional Fund Managers Initiative.
To date, around RM300 million has been mobilised under the programme, alongside more than RM30 million from external co-investors.
Khazanah is also targeting mid-tier companies, which contribute about 36 per cent of Malaysia's growth and 16 per cent of employment.
These firms often face challenges in accessing growth capital and scaling their operations.
Through programmes such as the Mid-Tier Company Growth Innovation Programme and Elevate, Khazanah has supported more than 50 Malaysian mid-tier companies.
"The missing middle matters because Malaysia cannot rely only on large incumbents or early-stage startups. We need more firms capable of scaling, innovating and competing internationally," Hisham added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN-PH ties unchanged despite Johor contest strategy, says Zahid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi says the coalition's decision to contest the Johor election independently does not signal any change in its relationship with Pakatan Harapan (PH).
He said the move should not be interpreted as a shift in political alignment.
Zahid said state politics should not be read as a reflection of national trends, citing differing circumstances in Johor, Melaka, Pahang and Perak.
"There is no early signal of anything because we must differentiate between state and federal governments, especially in states where the menteri besar or chief minister is from BN," he told Sin Chew Daily.
Asked whether BN's decision in Johor suggested a potential shift in national cooperation, he said it was normal for parties to contest independently at state level.
He said BN and PH had signed 10 cooperation agreements and agreed to maintain their partnership until the end of the current government's term.
"We agreed to cooperate until the end of the term, and this has been accepted by PH, BN, Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) and Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS)," he added.
Zahid said that future political arrangements would be reviewed after the current term ends, describing this as standard practice in coalition governments globally.
On the return of party veterans to Umno, Zahid said the party's priority was strengthening internal unity and that such developments should not be misinterpreted.
He was commenting on speculation over the return of former Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein and former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin.
Zahid said internal challenges were common in any political party, including factional differences and leadership disputes.
"Every party has internal issues. That is inevitable. We know that in other parties, some former vice-presidents have even gone on to form new parties. Umno has faced similar challenges.
"The most important thing is to strengthen internal unity and not misinterpret the significance of senior leaders returning to Umno," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Golden Land, Maple Hospitality to introduce MapleSuites brand in Sabah project
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Golden Land Bhd (GLB) has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Maple Hospitality Group (MHG), introducing the MapleSuites brand in Sabah through its upcoming project, The Playground @ Kota Kinabalu.
Under the exclusive collaboration, The Playground @ Kota Kinabalu will be the only project in Sabah operating under the MapleSuites brand, positioning the development as a distinctive lifestyle-driven hospitality destination in the state that integrates modern short-stay living, lifestyle experiences, and long-term investment value.
The partnership marks a significant milestone for both companies, combining GLB's aspirational development vision with MHG's hospitality management expertise to create an integrated hospitality ecosystem in Kota Kinabalu while enhancing the long-term value proposition of GLB's Sabah assets.
"The Playground @ Kota Kinabalu reflects our aspiration to create a development that goes beyond conventional real estate by integrating culture, lifestyle, hospitality, and long-term value creation," said Stewart Yap, chief executive officer of GLB.
"By aligning with a strong hospitality operator such as MHG, we are able to enhance the long-term value proposition of our Sabah assets while strengthening our presence in East Malaysia," he said.
Purpose-built to cater to the growing demand for urban short-stay living, The Playground @ Kota Kinabalu is designed as a commercial suites development blending lifestyle, hospitality, and community-focused experiences. The project reflects GLB's broader expansion strategy in Sabah, underpinned by rising tourism demand, expanding business travel, and growing investor interest in serviced accommodation.
Founded in Sabah in 1982, GLB began its journey in the timber industry under the name Tanah Emas Telupid Sdn Bhd and later diversified into plantation operations in 1986, laying the foundation for its long-term growth and expansion. In 2016, GLB expanded into property development with the launch of Midtown Plaza Sandakan, marking the group's entry into the real estate sector.
Its development portfolio now includes Golden Gateway in Penang, D'Sini Residences in Setia Alam, Taman Lavender Residences in Bentong, and The Playground @ Kota Kinabalu.
MHG, Malaysia's largest short-stay property management company chain, currently manages more than 3,000 rooms across major tourism and business destinations in the country. It operates three hospitality brands under its ecosystem—MapleHome, MapleSuites, and MapleReserve.
"Sabah represents a strong growth corridor for the hospitality industry, driven by increasing tourism demand and rising interest in quality short-stay accommodation," said Danny Hor, co-founder of MHG.
"We believe strategic collaborations such as this allow us to deliver better hospitality experiences while creating long-term value for property owners, guests, and the wider tourism ecosystem," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Karangkraf delegation tours Media Prima HQ, reaffirms industry cooperation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A delegation from Karangkraf Media Group today visited Media Prima Bhd's new headquarters in Anjung Liku, Balai Berita, Bangsar, to strengthen ties between two of Malaysia's leading media organisations.
The delegation was led by Karangkraf Group chairman Firdaus Hussamuddin and included Sinar Karangkraf chief executive officer Farah Hussamuddin, Karangkraf Group executive director Akmal Eirfan Fauzi, Ultimate Print chief executive officer Azizi Fickry, and senior representatives from Sinar Karangkraf, Sinar Harian, Sinar Daily, Nu Ideaktiv, Buku Karangkraf and Ultimate Print.
They were received by Media Prima group managing director Datuk Rafiq Razali, group chief financial officer Rosli Sabarudin, Media Prima Audio chief executive officer Nazri Noran, Media Prima News and Current Affairs Division group managing editor Jasbant Singh, and other members of the company's senior management team.
The visit included a tour of key operations at Media Prima's headquarters, including Media Prima Audio, the News and Current Affairs Division, the Malaysia Hari Ini (MHI) and Wanita Hari Ini (WHI) studios, REV Media Group, the News and Current Affairs editorial floor, and the KLIK newsroom.
Firdaus said the visit provided an opportunity for both organisations to exchange ideas and learn from one another.
"Media Prima has moved into a new building and I believe there are many new things we can learn and observe here.
"At the same time, we can share experiences with the Media Prima team and strengthen relationships between media practitioners," she said.
Firdaus said cooperation among media organisations was becoming increasingly important as the industry navigated rapid changes in the digital landscape.
"In today's environment, all media organisations must be united because we have a great responsibility to inform the public and represent the voices of the people.
"We must continue producing quality stories and content for our audiences," she said.
Asked whether the visit could pave the way for future collaborations between Karangkraf and Media Prima, Firdaus said the possibility remained open.
"That is something we can discuss in the future. Today's visit is mainly to get to know each other better," she said.
Meanwhile, Rafiq said Media Prima regularly engaged with industry partners and welcomed opportunities to strengthen cooperation within the local media ecosystem.
"All media players in Malaysia need to work together. We are not just competitors. Our real competitors are the international platforms.
"As local media organisations, we must collaborate to ensure we can continue producing content for Malaysians," he said.
The visit concluded with an exchange of commemorative gifts between both organisations.
Media Prima presented special framed copies of the New Straits Times featuring the birth dates of Karangkraf's chairman and executive director, while Karangkraf presented souvenirs to Media Prima as a token of appreciation.
Founded in 1978, Karangkraf is one of Malaysia's leading media and publishing groups, with interests spanning publishing, digital media and printing through brands including Sinar Harian, Sinar Daily, Buku Karangkraf, Nu Ideaktiv and Ultimate Print.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: NGOs raise alarm over rising hate speech against Rohingya refugees
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A coalition of civil society organisations has expressed alarm over what it described as a growing wave of hate speech, misinformation and intimidation targeting the Rohingya refugee community in Malaysia.
The groups said false narratives circulating on social media, including claims that Rohingya refugees are demanding citizenship, political power or territorial autonomy, have been deliberately amplified to incite fear and hostility.
They warned that the impact is no longer confined to the digital sphere.
"Community leaders have reported receiving threats, while personal information and home addresses are being circulated online. Families are living in fear of harassment and violence.
"Such actions put already vulnerable people at grave risk and have no place in a society that prides itself on compassion and justice," they said in a joint statement today.
The statement was endorsed by more than 20 civil society organisations, including Beyond Borders, Tenaganita, North South Initiative, Persatuan Sahabat Wanita Selangor, Centre for Independent Journalism, Geutanyoe Aceh, Women's Peace Network, Youth Empowerment Support, Suara Rakyat Malaysia, Instant Cafe Theatre, SIS Forum, Undi18, ALTSEAN-Burma and Asia Justice and Rights.
Others include Sarawak Women for Women, Family Frontiers, Pusat Komas, Bersih, Freedom Film Network, the Socialist Party of Malaysia, Iman Research, Asylum Access Malaysia and the Rohingya Maìyafuìnor Collaborative Network.
The groups said Rohingya refugees came to Malaysia not in search of privilege, but safety, after fleeing persecution in Myanmar.
They said responding to survivors of such circumstances with mockery, threats and dehumanisation betrayed the values of humanity and decency that Malaysians claim to uphold.
While Malaysia is not a signatory to the 1951 Refugee Convention, the coalition said this does not place the country outside international responsibility.
It said Malaysia remains bound by international human rights obligations through treaties it has ratified, including commitments to uphold human dignity, prevent discrimination, and protect the rights and welfare of children.
"Public discourse has become saturated with narratives that portray an entire community as criminals, invaders or threats based solely on their identity.
"Such rhetoric strips people of their humanity and creates an environment where discrimination and violence become acceptable," the statement said.
The groups called on public officials, political actors, media organisations and social media platforms to reject and actively counter misinformation rather than exploit public anger and fear.
They also urged law enforcement agencies to take threats against refugees seriously.
"Malaysians must resist efforts to pit communities against one another through lies and scapegoating.
"The measure of a nation is not how it treats the powerful, but how it treats the vulnerable.
"At a time when hatred is being amplified, we call on all Malaysians to choose humanity over hostility, truth over misinformation, and solidarity over fear," they said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Putrajaya urged to tax sweetened condensed milk to curb NCD crisis
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Putrajaya should introduce a specific excise tax on sweetened condensed milk and sweetened creamer, expanding the national "War against Sugar" campaign under the 2027 Budget.
Health think-tank Galen Centre for Health and Social Policy chief executive officer Azrul Mohd Khalib said the existing sugar-sweetened beverage tax alone was insufficient to address the country's growing non-communicable diseases (NCD) crisis.
He said sweetened condensed milk remained one of the most common, normalised and often overlooked sources of added sugar in the Malaysian diet.
"The Galen Centre urges the Finance Ministry and the Health Ministry to include a condensed milk and sweetened creamer excise tax in the 2027 federal budget.
"The Galen Centre proposes that the government introduce a tiered excise tax on sweetened condensed milk, sweetened creamer and similar high-sugar milk-based products, based on sugar content per 100g," he said in a statement today.
Azrul said products with higher sugar content should be taxed at a higher rate, while lower-sugar reformulated products should attract a lower rate to encourage manufacturers to reduce sugar levels and offer healthier alternatives.
He said the tax should apply not only to retail products sold in supermarkets but also to commercial and bulk supplies used by eateries, bakeries and food manufacturers.
Azrul said a glass of teh tarik or coffee sold at many eateries may contain several spoonfuls of condensed milk, adding significantly to daily sugar intake.
He said Malaysia was facing a worsening NCD crisis, with 54.4 per cent of adults overweight or obese and nearly 2.3 million people living with three NCDs simultaneously, according to the 2023 National Health and Morbidity Survey.
NCDs cost the country an estimated RM64.2 billion in 2021, equivalent to 4.2 per cent of gross domestic product, he added.
He said health policies must therefore place greater emphasis on prevention rather than to continue relying on costly treatment and disease management.
"This is not about attacking food culture. It is about recognising that the sugar burden in our drinks and food has changed the health of the nation.
"Good food and drink should not come with a future of diabetes, kidney failure, blindness, amputations and heart disease.
"Every ringgit not spent on prevention today will be paid many times over in dialysis, hospital admissions, heart procedures, medicines, disability, lost productivity and premature death tomorrow," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jaslinda's going home: Rescued hiker discharged from hospital
Article:
TAPAH: Hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who was found safe after spending two weeks lost on Gunung Batu Putih, was discharged from Tapah Hospital today after three days of treatment.
Jaslinda was seen leaving the women's ward at 1.50pm, accompanied by her husband, Haszman Othman, 61, and healthcare personnel, before the couple left in a Honda City.
Using a walking stick and with her left leg bandaged, Jaslinda thanked Malaysians for their prayers and those involved in the search and rescue operation.
"Alhamdulillah, I am healthy. Thank you very much to all Malaysians for praying for my safety. I truly appreciate it," she said.
Jaslinda also apologised to search personnel and others involved in efforts to locate her.
[[nid:1458953]]
"Thank you everyone. I am sorry for troubling the search and rescue team. I had no intention of troubling anyone," she said.
When asked about allegations surrounding her disappearance, Jaslinda said she would address the claims at a later date.
"I will issue a statement later. What people have heard are all lies. I will give my statement later," she said.
Jaslinda was found on Saturday by two Orang Asli from Kampung Lubuk Gaharu, Pos Musoh, who were fishing along Sungai Woh, 14 days after she went missing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Alibaba Cloud launches Johor cloud region, boosts Malaysia AI push
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Alibaba Cloud has launched a new public cloud region in Johor, supported by two new data centres as it continues to strengthen its cloud and artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure in Malaysia amid growing demand for digital and AI services.
The expansions form part of the company's previously announced US$53 billion investment in AI and cloud infrastructure and bring Alibaba Cloud's global network to 104 availability zones across 32 regions.
The new Johor facilities provide a broad range of cloud services such as computing, storage, networking, databases, cybersecurity, big data and cloud-native solutions.
Alibaba Cloud plans to also introduce a suite of agentic AI services in Malaysia later this year to support enterprises developing and deploying AI agents at scale.
With the addition of the Johor region, Alibaba Cloud now operates five data centres in Malaysia, marking its largest infrastructure footprint in Southeast Asia.
The company first entered the Malaysian market in 2017 and established its first twin data centre presence before adding a third facility in 2025.
Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said the new investment further strengthens Malaysia's position as a regional hub for AI-ready infrastructure and high-value digital investments.
"The expansion aligns with Malaysia's cloud-first and AI-driven national agenda, propelling us closer to becoming an AI Nation by 2030 through secure, scalable and future-ready digital infrastructure," he said.
Alibaba Cloud Intelligence general manager for Malaysia Choong Hon Keat said the latest expansion was driven by rising customer demand as businesses increasingly adopt cloud-native operations and integrate AI into their operations.
"Our new data centres in Johor will enable us to deliver more resilient, low-latency services, helping companies innovate faster, scale securely, and operate with greater efficiency in the AI era," he added.
Several Malaysian companies are already leveraging Alibaba Cloud's infrastructure and AI technologies.
TNG Digital is using the company's unified data platform to enhance search and recommendation capabilities on TNG eWallet, while YTL AI Labs has deepened its collaboration with Alibaba Cloud to develop the ILMU family of AI models and applications, advancing Malay-language AI capabilities through cloud-based infrastructure and tools.
Creative technology firms Morphyx.io and TabSpace.ai are also using Alibaba Cloud's AI video generation models and large language models to streamline content production, reduce costs and accelerate the creation of digital assets.
Alibaba Cloud said the new Johor region will strengthen resilience, reduce latency and enhance disaster recovery capabilities across Malaysia and Southeast Asia, supporting businesses as AI and cloud adoption continue to accelerate.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Missile maker Kongsberg seeks talks with Malaysia over NSM dispute
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Norwegian defence firm Kongsberg Defence and Aerospace (KDA) has sought discussions with Malaysia over the latter's compensation claims following the collapse of the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) procurement deal.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the ministry had received a request from the company, but stressed that Malaysia would continue pursuing its claims over Kongsberg's failure to fulfil the contract.
"We have received a request from the company to hold discussions. However, our claims (for compensation) are still ongoing.
"We have not met yet and, so far, we do not know what they want to discuss, but we have received a letter from them," he said at a press conference during the Armed Forces Education Excellence Awards ceremony at Wisma Perwira.
In April, Norway withdrew several export licences related to certain technologies as part of efforts to tighten the country's export controls, affecting the NSM procurement agreement between the
Royal Malaysian Navy and Kongsberg,
the manufacturer of the missile system.
Following the move, Malaysia issued a notice of demand to the Norwegian defence firm over the cancellation of the export licence for the NSM system intended for the RMN's Littoral Combat Ship and Lekiu-class frigates,
claiming losses
exceeding RM1 billion.
The government is seeking compensation for both direct and indirect losses arising from Norway's unilateral decision to revoke the export licence for the defence system.
Malaysia had already paid €126 million (RM583 million), equivalent to 95 per cent of the contract value, before the cancellation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Short, sharp and more strategic': EU's new approach to climate talks
Article:
THE EU wants to target fewer, clearer goals at the world's annual climate summit in November, building alliances in advance to avoid a repeat of last year's bruising talks where the bloc struggled to advance its agenda, a document showed on Monday.
The internal document, which was seen by Reuters, was prepared by the upcoming Irish presidency of the 27-nation European Union and spells out the strategy the EU should take for the United Nations' COP31 climate summit in Turkiye.
The EU's negotiating mandate should be "shorter, sharper and more strategic" than in previous years, the document said.
"We should say fewer things, more clearly - and stand firmly behind them," it said.
The previous global climate conference, COP30, in Brazil, ended without deals on EU priorities to accelerate cuts to planet-heating emissions and reduce fossil fuel use.
"Europe should continue to stand firmly for ambitious climate action and for the integrity of the multilateral process. But ambition alone is not a strategy," the document said.
While discussions of a roadmap to phase out fossil fuels and plans to increase climate funding dominated the talks in Brazil last year, the 15-page EU negotiating mandate that its member countries had agreed ahead of the summit did not include positions on those issues.
Some diplomats said the EU's failure to push through its agenda was in part due to a lack of preparation.
The challenge to agree on ambitious climate action at this year's COP comes as countries around the world struggle to respond to the Iran war's disruption of energy supplies, with some expanding renewable energy while others burn more coal.
Ireland will also focus on early outreach by EU member governments to other countries, building cooperation with both allies and opponents in the talks, the document said.
This will involve dividing up negotiating responsibilities among member states' ministers to ensure they are "deployed strategically both in the lead-up to and during COP31 itself."
"Political ownership matters. Ministers should not arrive at COP only to react to events as they unfold," the document said.
Asked about the document, a spokesperson for Ireland's climate ministry told Reuters the country was taking a streamlined approach to preparing for COP, and "concentrating our efforts where we can make the greatest contribution and on key priorities."
"Climate diplomacy is not just about two weeks at a COP; it is a year-round process of engagement, relationship-building and delivery," the spokesperson said.
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesian rupiah strengthens after surprise BI rate hike
Article:
JAKARTA: The Indonesian rupiah firmed after the central bank unexpectedly raised interest rates on Tuesday, a week ahead of its scheduled policy meeting, as it sought to put a floor under the free-falling currency against growing external pressures.
The rupiah appreciated to 18,050 per dollar immediately after the surprise quarter-point rate hike, just three weeks after Bank Indonesia hiked its policy interest rate by 50 basis points.
"The move does show a sense of proactiveness in preventing any further backsliding in sentiment," said Lavanya Venkateswaran, a senior Asean economist at OCBC.
"It can help stabilise sentiment at the margin. However, its sustainability will also depend on clearer policy direction of the broader authorities to address perceived risks around recent announcements."
Southeast Asia's biggest economy has been facing declining investor confidence over fiscal concerns, equity market governance issues highlighted by an MSCI review, risks to central bank autonomy, and a shifting commodity export policy.
The Jakarta Composite Index was up 4.8 per cent at 5,599.74 points prior to the midday break. The benchmark rose as much as 5.3 per cent earlier in the day after tumbling around 4.5 per cent in the previous session.
Equities in other Asian emerging markets bounced back as investors rushed into battered equities after Iran and Israel said they had halted attacks, with AI-linked stocks in South Korea and Taiwan rebounding after a sharp correction on Monday.
Currencies also found support as oil prices eased: the South Korean won appreciated to 1,511 per dollar, while the Malaysian ringgit firmed to 4.055 a dollar, recovering from a five-month low. The MSCI EM Asia equities index jumped four per cent, reversing much of the decline from Monday.
A gauge tracking Asean stocks also rebounded from an eight-month low hit in the previous session. South Korea's benchmark KOSPI index rebounded sharply on the day, surging eight per cent and triggering a sidecar mechanism within minutes of opening.
The index ended more than eight per cent in the red in the previous session on rising bets of US rate hikes. Chipmakers SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics soared about 15 per cent and 9 per cent, respectively.
The KOSPI, despite a 15 per cent decline over the past three sessions, remains around 90 per cent in the green this year, largely driven by leveraged equity investment by retail investors, which has topped a record 60 trillion won as of the end of May, according to a Bank of Korea report.
Equities in Taiwan gained as much as three per cent on Tuesday, and are up 54 per cent for the year. Top contract chipmaker TSMC has advanced 49 per cent so far this year.
In Southeast Asia, Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index, Thailand's SET index , and the Philippine benchmark index advanced more than one per cent each, while stocks in Malaysia erased early gains to trade flat.
Currencies were largely muted: the Philippine peso, the Singapore dollar, and the Indian rupee inched higher, while the Thai baht slipped marginally to 32.875 a dollar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Several actions' to follow after Umno-Pas talks, says Zahid
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said several actions should follow recent talks between his party and Pas.
Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, said the meeting, which he described as a positive step, focused on resolving non-political matters.
He said any political matters arising from the discussions would be refined in line with Umno's commitment to the unity government.
"That is our foundation and that foundation must be respected — but the door must also be opened for us to hold discussions and explore new approaches should the political dynamics shift at some point in the future," he said.
Zahid expressed confidence that any outstanding differences between the two parties could be overcome.
"I am confident that there is no problem that cannot be resolved, no difference of opinion that cannot be reconciled," he told reporters after the 2026 Community Development Department Educators' Day Celebration.
However, Zahid did not elaborate on the actions he was referring to.
On Friday, Zahid said the meeting between Umno and Pas leaders the night before was part of routine discussions on potential cooperation aimed at preserving political stability.
He said the meeting
did not involve
the party presidents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teacher claims trial to five counts of sexually assaulting student, two pornography charges
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A teacher was charged in two Sessions Courts and a magistrate's court here today with five counts of physical sexual assault against his female student and two counts of possessing pornography.
The accused, Mohamad Amirul Firdaus Awang Meran, 28, pleaded not guilty to all charges.
The accused pleaded not guilty before Sessions Court judge Nor Aziati Jaafar to three charges of physical sexual assault against an 11-year-old girl while holding a position of trust as her teacher.
The offences were allegedly committed in a Perodua Bezza at a playground car park in Masai between 7pm on Feb 26, 2024, and 8am on Aug 18, 2024.
He pleaded not guilty before Sessions Court judge Madihah Zainol to two similar charges involving the same victim, who was 12 years old at the time of the alleged offences.
The offences were allegedly committed in the same vehicle at the same location at 8.30pm on May 16, 2025, and 8pm on Aug 27, 2025.
He was charged under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, read together with Section 16(1) of the same Act.
Under Section 14(a), the accused faces a maximum jail term of 20 years and whipping. Under Section 16(1), he faces an additional jail term of up to five years and not fewer than two strokes of the rotan upon conviction.
He was charged before magistrate Atifah Hazimah Wahab with two counts of possessing obscene material in two mobile phones. The offences were allegedly committed along Jalan Seri Wangi 1/1, Taman Perindustrian Plentong, Masai, at 10.30pm on May 21, 2026.
The charges were framed under Section 292(a) of the Penal Code, which provides for a maximum jail term of three years, a fine, or both, upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutors Nur Farah Wahida Shahudin, S. Manushri and Sarah Siti Aisyah Mustapha Kamal respectively proposed bail of RM20,000 with conditions.
Defence counsel Nur Zafirah Norizan sought a lower bail amount, saying the accused had served as a teacher for five years and currently teaches at a primary school in Masai.
She said the accused earned a net monthly income of RM3,000 and supported his parents, aged 50 and 60, who live in Terengganu, and suffer from nerve-related illnesses.
She added that the accused is the eldest of three siblings, supports a younger sibling who is still schooling, and has been diagnosed with moderate depressive disorder.
Judge Nor Aziati allowed bail at RM10,000, judge Madihah set bail at RM3,000, while magistrate Atifah Hazimah fixed bail at RM6,000.
The courts also imposed three additional conditions. The accused is barred from contacting the victim and witnesses until the disposal of the case, must report to the nearest police station once a month, and must surrender his international passport to the court.
The courts fixed July 13 for mention and document submission.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nine witnesses questioned in alleged slap case involving uni student, politician
Article:
KUANTAN: Pahang police have recorded statements from nine individuals as part of investigations into a university student's claim that he was slapped by a senator in Bentong on May 20.
Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said statements had been recorded from witnesses, including the politician and the 23-year-old victim, who is the grandson of former Kelantan menteri besar Tan Sri Nik Abdul Aziz Nik Mat.
"The probe is still ongoing...once completed, the investigation paper will be referred to the deputy public prosecutor," he said.
Yahaya was responding to a question about the progress of the investigation into the alleged slapping incident, which occurred during a university programme in Janda Baik, Bentong on May 20.
Both the suspect, and the victim,
who did not suffer serious injuries,
lodged separate police reports.
The case is being investigated under Section 323 of the Penal Code, which provides for imprisonment of up to one year, a fine of up to RM2,000, or both, upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Philippines takes diplomatic action against China over floating structure in South China Sea
Article:
MANILA: The Philippines' foreign ministry has undertaken appropriate diplomatic action against China in connection with the "illegal presence" of a floating structure in a disputed atoll, the country's South China Sea task force said on Tuesday.
The task force confirmed the presence of a floating structure measuring six by six metres and featuring what appeared to be an antenna in the disputed Scarborough Shoal in the South China Sea.
"The Philippine government will continue to take appropriate actions consistent with international law and in defence of the country's national interests," it said in a statement, adding that the protection of Manila's sovereignty and jurisdiction "remains a paramount consideration."
The structure, initially spotted at the entrance to the shoal and later inside its lagoon, appeared to be manned, the task force said, citing the latest images from the armed forces.
The task force did not say when the images were taken, but Defence Secretary Gilberto Teodoro said on the sidelines of the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore on May 30 that he had received raw information indicating the presence of the structure on the shoal.
The task force said it is closely monitoring developments and assessing the nature, purpose and potential implications of the installation, insisting that Scarborough "is part of the Philippines and within its maritime zones as recognised by international law."
Satellite images obtained by Reuters on June 5 confirmed the presence of a structure at the lagoon's entrance, but later images showed it was no longer present.
Scarborough is a prime fishing spot located 200 km (124 miles) off the western coast of the country. It is also 874 km from Hainan, China's nearest land mass.
Since 2012, China has maintained constant deployment of coastguard and maritime militia ships in the area.
Sovereignty over Scarborough, which China calls Huangyan Island, has not been established, but a 2016 arbitral tribunal ruled largely in favour of the Philippines, saying China's blockade of the shoal violated international law and that the area was a traditional fishing ground for several countries.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia raises rates in surprise move to support sinking rupiah
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia's central bank raised interest rates by 25 basis points on Tuesday in an unexpected off-cycle rate hike, taking the benchmark rate to 5.50 per cent as it moves to stabilise the rupiah.
Bank Indonesia (BI) said in a statement posted on its website that the off-cycle rate hike was necessary because "the rupiah exchange rate has weakened more than expected" since its last meeting.
The rupiah has been under mounting pressure for weeks, and is one of the world's worst performing currencies, falling 8 per cent this year and 7 per cent since the Iran war erupted.
The currencystrengthened slightly to 18,085 after the decision, a day after touching a record low of 18,190 per US dollar. In the past three weeks it experienced its steepest drop since 2020.
BI hiked rates by a higher than expected 50 basis points in its last meeting in May. But even that hike and a USUS$12 billion drop this year in Indonesia's foreign exchange reserves, which the central bank uses to defend the rupiah, have not been able to shore up the currency.
"This rate hike is a further step to strengthen the rupiah's exchange rate stabilisation against the impact of high global volatility caused by the war in the Middle East and a preemptive measure to maintain inflation in 2026 and 2027 within the target range," BI said.
"This policy is also intended to increase returns by attracting foreign portfolio investment inflows into Indonesia," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: Pas–Bersatu split shows alliances need sincerity, not convenience
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Umno had anticipated Pas' decision to end its cooperation with Bersatu, with party president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi describing the split as a lesson that political alliances must be built on sincerity rather than convenience.
Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, said political cooperation could not be sustained by calculations alone and must be anchored in a long-term vision for national unity and development.
"We had anticipated this would happen. We are not pre-empting God's will, but from a political perspective, any collaboration must be founded on complete sincerity and free from elements of betrayal.
"It must be for the long term so that the nation's goals can be achieved and the unity of the people realised," he said.
He told reporters this after the 2026 Community Development Department (Kemas) Educators' Day celebration at the Rural and Regional Development Ministry today.
Zahid also issued a veiled warning over any future political arrangements arising from the split, saying commitments must go beyond symbolic gestures.
"Whatever steps are taken after this, documents and pledges must not merely be signed. Most importantly, sincerity must be translated into something lasting and enduring, InsyaAllah," he said.
Yesterday, Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang announced the
termination of the party's political cooperation
with Bersatu following a special meeting of its Central Working Committee.
However, Pas information chief Ahmad Fadhli Shaari said the party would
remain within Perikatan Nasional
despite ending ties with Bersatu.
The split follows months of
tension between the two parties,
including an alleged move by Bersatu to block the admission of several Malay-Muslim parties into the opposition coalition.
Following the decision, Hadi said Pas would explore new forms of political understanding and electoral cooperation aimed at strengthening Muslim unity ahead of the upcoming state elections and the 16th general election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Metal‑cutting caused former Odeon Cinema blaze in Penang that killed Myanmar worker, probe finds
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, June 9 — A fire that destroyed an entertainment centre in the former Odeon Cinema building on Jalan Penang here on May 30, killing a Myanmar national, is believed to have been caused by metal cutting work using oxy-acetylene equipment.
Penang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) director Mohamad Shoki Hamzah said forensic investigations found the fire was accidentally triggered during renovation works at the premises.
“CCTV footage showed two of the three workers carrying out metal cutting work using an oxy-cutter set before hot molten metal residue fell onto combustible materials below and ignited a fire.
“Investigations also found that the fire originated on the ground floor at the rear section of the premises, based on the account of a surviving witness,” he told Bernama today.
Mohamad Shoki said inspections by the department’s K9 Detection Unit found no traces of fire accelerants at the scene.
He said the fire involved a two-storey heritage building with a floor area of about 900 square metres, adding that the value of the property and losses incurred were still being assessed.
In the incident, firefighters received an emergency call at 8.50 pm on May 30. The body of construction worker Mohamad Boshi Sabi Ullah, 56, was found on the ground floor near the building’s front entrance with first-degree burn injuries.
Timur Laut deputy police chief Supt Lee Swee Sake previously said the case had been classified as a sudden death report, with investigations finding no elements of foul play.
A post-mortem examination determined that the victim died from smoke inhalation. — Bernama

Title: Sarawak Water: Tests tonight to trace major leak and determine whether 20,000 or 70,000 Miri consumers face water outage
Article:
MIRI, June 9 — Sarawak Water Sdn Bhd will begin preliminary testing on affected transmission pipelines at 10pm tonight to identify the source of a major leak here.
Sarawak Water acting chief executive officer Malseni Jamal said the assessment would involve temporarily shutting off a 750mm valve to establish whether the leak originates from the 750mm pipeline or the larger 1.1 metre pipeline.
“If the leak is traced to the 750mm pipeline, approximately 20,000 households, representing about 30 per cent of consumers in Miri could be affected when repair works commence on Wednesday (June 10), with valve closure scheduled for 5pm,” she told a press conference.
Malseni said if the leak is traced to the 750mm pipeline, the areas affected would be Taman Jelita, Taman Tunku, Jalan Engkabang, Sungai Rait, Eastwood, Hilltop, and Riam.
However, if the leak is from the 1.1 metre pipeline, more than 70,000 consumers, which is equivalent to 70 per cent of the city’s population, could be affected by a supply interruption.
This would affect areas such as Jalan Krokop, Jalan Bulan Sabit, Pulau Melayu, Permyjaya, South Lake, Tudan Senadin, Lutong, Kuala Baram, Kuala Masjid, and Sungai Tujoh.
Other affected areas would include Kampung Beraya, Kuala Sibuti, Bukit Song, Sungai Karab, Bukit Peninjau, Beluru town area, and Sungai Seruas.
“It might not be that vast. We will know by tonight after we temporarily close the valve to check. From there we will find the cause and will update our social media, which is our communication channel,” she added. — The Borneo Post

Title: Penang CM says rise in state reserves predates quit rent increase
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, June 9 — Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow today dismissed claims that the state’s cash reserves had risen to more than RM600 million because of the recent quit rent hike, saying the increase was driven by a surplus recorded in 2025.
He said the balance in the state’s Consolidated Revenue Account had already increased before the revised quit rent rates came into effect on January 1 this year.
“We already saw an increase in the reserves before the new quit rent rates came into effect on January 1, 2026,” he told reporters at a press conference here.
Chow said the state recorded a surplus in 2025, with cash reserves exceeding RM500 million as of December 31, 2025.
“This is not related to the quit rent increase. When did the increase take effect? January 1, 2026. The surplus has nothing to do with the quit rent hike,” he said.
His remarks come after allegations that Penang’s cash reserves had declined in 2024, resulting in a deficit.
Responding to the criticism yesterday, Chow said the state’s reserves in the Consolidated Revenue Account had increased to RM616 million in the first five months of this year.
Following that, critics alleged that the increase in state revenue was achieved through taxes, additional charges and tariffs that burdened the public.
Chow, however, said the state government had not projected substantial additional revenue from the revised quit rent rates.
“I already announced in the state assembly that if we get RM50 million, I would already be very happy,” he said.

Title: Budget 2027 to stay development‑driven amid crisis‑year pressures, says Akmal Nasrullah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — The Ministry of Economy will continue to focus on development expenditure in Budget 2027, which is expected to be tabled this October, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.
He said all ministries have submitted their development proposals, and evaluations are being conducted at the ministry level through a bidding mechanism.
“But at the same time, we acknowledge that 2026 is a year in which we must manage the economy in a crisis situation. Therefore, in preparing for 2027, the Ministry of Economy is looking at the situation from a development aspect.
“The engagement process must be carried out as thoroughly as possible. To date, all ministries have already submitted their development planning proposals for 2027,” he told reporters after the Ministerial session at Invest Malaysia here today.
Akmal Nasrullah said a detailed review of those development plans will begin next month.
He said that the government remains committed to implementing the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP), including a development expenditure target of around RM619 billion over the plan’s five-year implementation period.
Regarding the influx of e-commerce products from China and the participation of foreign businesses in the local market, the minister said the government must strike a balance between facilitating business and ensuring fair competition for local players.
He said that while the ease of doing business is important, the government must prevent unhealthy competition that could harm local businesses.
“From one perspective, ease of doing business is crucial to enabling economic activities to run smoothly. But at the same time, we must manage it so that unhealthy competition does not occur,” he said.
Akmal Nasrullah said the issue had been presented to the Cabinet, which agreed to set up a special committee led by the Ministry of Finance to study the matter thoroughly, as it involves various ministries and agencies.
“Among the ministries involved are the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living, which is responsible for consumer matters, and the Ministry of Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives, which handles the development of small and medium enterprises,” he added. — Bernama

Title: Chow: Penang seeking DoE input before deciding on Jelutong landfill rehabilitation project
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, June 9 — The Penang government has met the Department of Environment (DoE) to better understand the environmental impact assessment (EIA) considerations for the proposed Jelutong landfill rehabilitation and Karpal Singh Drive reclamation project, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today.
He said the discussion with the DoE is part of an ongoing process before any decision is made on the project.
“We have met with the Department of Environment to understand the considerations regarding the EIA application for the project site. This process is still ongoing,” he told reporters after delivering his keynote address at the Penang Career Discovery Day x Penang HR Forward Forum 2026 here.
Chow said the state government’s decision-making process must be guided by verified information and proper procedures.
“We know there are NGOs that are impatient and have made various allegations against the state government,” he said.
“But I always stress that the decision-making process must be preceded by proper consideration of accurate information,” he added.
He said the state government must follow the proper process as any decision would have implications, particularly financial ones.
Recently, non-governmental organisation Protect Karpal questioned whether the developer of the Jelutong Landfill Joint Development Agreement (JDA) was receiving “exceptional accommodations”.
The group raised concerns over repeated extensions of time (EOT), revisions to payment structures and the scale of the proposed coastal reclamation plan.
It also questioned whether financial restructuring and continued extensions were justified despite repeated EIA rejections and delays in the commencement of the project.
Chow said the DoE has accepted another EIA application submitted by the developer.
“The state environment exco met with the DoE yesterday and he will present what was discussed to the state exco tomorrow,” he said.

Title: Mukah police seek public help to find two 14‑year‑old girls missing since Sunday
Article:
MUKAH, June 9 — Police are seeking help from the public to locate two 14-year-old schoolgirls who were reported missing yesterday.
Mukah police chief Muhamad Rizal Alias said Easter Agnes David and Frocilea Rajok Jessica were last seen in Mukah town on Sunday at around 1.30pm.
He said the schoolmates are believed to have left with other friends after allegedly refusing to register at their school hostel.
“The two missing girls are identified as Easter Agnes David, an Iban girl from Rumah Jali at Batu 15, Jalan Selangau-Mukah, while Frocilea Rajok Jessica is from Rumah Nelson Chuat Begeri, KM59 Jalan Mukah-Selangau,” he said in a statement today.
